content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

work on sd burrow-6a65. use ml. commit when done.
I'll start by priming the project context and looking at the issue.

arguments.command: cd /workspace && ml prime 2>&1 | head -100; echo "---ISSUE---"; sd show burrow-6a65 2>&1

> Manifest mode lists available domains. Load records on demand with `ml prime <domain>` or `ml prime --files <path>`.

## Quick Reference

- `ml prime <domain>` — load full records for one domain
- `ml prime --files <path>` — load records relevant to specific files
- `ml search "<query>"` — search records across domains
- `ml record <domain> --type <type> --description "..."` — store an insight
- `ml learn` — discover what to record from changed files
- `ml sync` — validate, stage, and commit .mulch/ changes

content.text:
pl-a456  approved  rev 1
Name:     Wire resumeOfRunId seam end-to-end for session resume
Seed:     burrow-89cb
Template: feature
Created:  2026-06-05T22:11:48.210Z
Updated:  2026-06-05T22:11:48.210Z
Review suggested (no reviewer recorded yet)

Sections:
  context
    The resume machinery already exists but nothing wires it, so a run can never resume a prior session (GH #21). piRuntime.buildResumeCommand emits --session <id>; supportsResume is true (claude-code has --resume); extractMetadata captures the pi session_id into Run.metadataJson on exit 0; the resume_of_run_id column exists and RunsRepo.enqueue persists it. But dispatchRun (src/runner/dispatch.ts:131) only ever calls buildSpawnCommand; the createRun handler (src/server/handlers.ts:464) never reads resumeOfRunId from the body; CreateRunBodySchema (src/server/openapi/schemas.ts:369) doesn't document it; and neither RunCreateInput / client.runs.create (src/lib/client.ts:84,293) nor HttpRunCreateInput / the HTTP client create (src/lib/http-client.ts:91,511) forward it. Net effect: an HTTP or library caller cannot resume a session — every run spawns a fresh agent, so multi-turn agents that depend on session continuity can't be built on the public API. The repo owner decided to implement this end-to-end rather than wait for the issue author's promised PR; the issue author's analysis should be credited in the close comment.
  approach
    Wire the existing resumeOfRunId seam end to end across the three layers, ordered so each layer's input is available before the next consumes it. (1) API + schema + persistence: createRun reads optional resumeOfRunId from the body, threads it through RunCreateInput into the existing RunsRepo.enqueue seam (which already accepts and persists resume_of_run_id), and CreateRunBodySchema documents the field; regenerate the OpenAPI golden. (2) Clients: add resumeOfRunId to RunCreateInput (in-process) and HttpRunCreateInput (HTTP), forwarding it on the wire so HTTP/in-process parity holds. (3) Dispatcher routing: dispatchRun reads run.resumeOfRunId and, when set, validates eligibility (runtime supportsResume; prior run exists, succeeded, same burrowId, same agentId) then routes to runtime.buildResumeCommand(priorRun) instead of buildSpawnCommand — failing fast with a clear, structured errorMessage (same failed-with-message convention dispatchRun already uses for agent/burrow checks) on any ineligibility. This reuses every existing primitive (enqueue column, buildResumeCommand, supportsResume, the failed-with-message pattern at dispatch.ts:84-108) rather than introducing new storage or new runtime APIs. dispatcher.test.ts already asserts the resume argv shape directly via buildResumeCommand with a comment that auto-routing on the spawn path is separate scope — this work closes exactly that gap, so that comment is updated and happy-path + each eligibility-failure mode get dispatcher tests.
  alternatives
    1. name: Wait for the issue author's promised PR
       rejected_because: Repo owner explicitly decided to implement end-to-end ourselves; we credit the author's analysis in the close comment instead of depending on an external PR.
    2. name: Resume routing inside the runtime layer rather than dispatchRun
       rejected_because: dispatchRun is the single place that already chooses buildSpawnCommand and owns the burrow/agent/run context needed for eligibility checks; pushing routing into the runtime would duplicate that context and split the decision across layers.
  steps
    1. API: createRun handler reads resumeOfRunId, OpenAPI CreateRunBody documents it, persisted via RunsRepo.enqueue (regen golden)
       blocks: 2, 3
    2. Clients: RunCreateInput + HttpRunCreateInput carry resumeOfRunId; in-process create and HTTP client forward it (wire parity)
       blocks: 3
    3. Dispatcher: dispatchRun validates resume eligibility and routes to buildResumeCommand(priorRun) with fail-fast errors; tests for each failure mode + happy path; run quality gates
       blocks: 4
    4. Release + close GH #21: patch bump (version:bump patch), run .claude/commands/release.md flow, close issue 21 crediting the author's analysis
  risks
    1. Pi e2e dispatcher tests (src/runner/dispatcher.test.ts) are sensitive to workspacePath: a prior failure (mx-f267ec) had seedActiveBurrow use '/ws' which broke mkdir of /ws/.pi/sessions. New resume tests that seed a prior succeeded run must use a real host-writable workspacePath, not '/ws'.
    2. The OpenAPI golden (src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json) must be regenerated when CreateRunBodySchema changes, or the golden test fails CI.
    3. Eligibility must fail fast with a clear message (reusing the failed-with-message convention in dispatch.ts:84-108) rather than silently falling back to a fresh spawn, which would mask the caller's resume intent.
    4. FINAL step does a generic patch bump — another plan may also bump patch and ordering isn't guaranteed, so the resulting version number must not be hardcoded anywhere in the plan.
  acceptance
    1. POST /burrows/:id/runs accepts an optional resumeOfRunId; createRun reads it and it is persisted on the run row via RunsRepo.enqueue.
    2. CreateRunBodySchema documents resumeOfRunId and the OpenAPI golden (src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json) is regenerated to match.
    3. RunCreateInput (in-process) and HttpRunCreateInput (HTTP) both carry resumeOfRunId; client.runs.create and the HTTP client forward it with HTTP/in-process parity.
    4. When run.resumeOfRunId is set, dispatchRun validates eligibility (runtime supportsResume; prior run exists, succeeded, same burrowId, same agentId) and routes to buildResumeCommand(priorRun); otherwise it uses buildSpawnCommand.
    5. Each eligibility failure mode (runtime lacks resume support, prior run missing, not succeeded, different burrow, different agent) returns a failed RunOutcome with a clear, distinct errorMessage and does NOT fall back to a fresh spawn.
    6. dispatcher.test.ts covers the happy resume path plus each eligibility failure mode, and the stale 'separate scope' comment is updated.
    7. bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass.
    8. A patch version bump is applied via bun run version:bump patch (package.json and src/index.ts VERSION in sync), the release flow per .claude/commands/release.md is followed, and GitHub issue #21 is closed with a comment linking the work and crediting the issue author's analysis.

Children (4):
  burrow-6a65  [open]  API: createRun handler reads resumeOfRunId, OpenAPI CreateRunBody documents it, persisted via RunsRepo.enqueue (regen golden)
  burrow-5704  [open]  Clients: RunCreateInput + HttpRunCreateInput carry resumeOfRunId; in-process create and HTTP client forward it (wire parity)
  burrow-c386  [open]  Dispatcher: dispatchRun validates resume eligibility and routes to buildResumeCommand(priorRun) with fail-fast errors; tests for each failure mode + happy path; run quality gates
  burrow-a57d  [open]  Release + close GH #21: patch bump (version:bump patch), run .claude/commands/release.md flow, close issue 21 crediting the author's analysis

## Quick Reference

- `ml search "query"` — find relevant records before implementing
- `ml prime --files src/foo.ts` — prime **before** editing a file, not just at session start
- `ml prime --context` — load records for git-changed files
- `ml record <domain> --type <type> --description "..."`
  - Evidence: commit + files auto-populate from git. Trackers: `--evidence-seeds` / `--evidence-gh` / `--evidence-linear` / `--evidence-bead`. Override commit: `--evidence-commit <sha>`. Link records: `--relates-to <mx-id>`
- `ml doctor` — check record health

**Record types and required flags:**

| Type | Required flags |
|------|----------------|
| `convention` | `"<content>"` (positional) |
| `pattern` | `--name "..." --description "..."` |
| `failure` | `--description "..." --resolution "..."` |
| `decision` | `--title "..." --rationale "..."` |
| `reference` | `--name "..." --description "..."` |
| `guide` | `--name "..." --description "..."` |

```bash
ml sync                               # validate, stage, commit
```

Skip if no insight surfaced. Unrecorded learnings are lost; ritual filler records are also noise.
